title: State machine: malformed Claude STATE fields corrupt booking flow silently
failing_behavior: Claude occasionally emits malformed STATE markers: wrong date format ('April 15' instead of '2026-04-15'), non-array services ('Gel Manicure' string instead of ['Gel Manicure']), invalid time ('2 PM' instead of '14:00'), or unknown pendingAction. _mergeState() accepted all values blindly via Object.keys() copy. Result: corrupted booking state that appears valid but breaks availability check, price lookup, or date display silently.
root_cause: _mergeState() used Object.keys(update).forEach(k => state[k] = update[k]) with zero validation. Any malformed field from Claude's STATE JSON was accepted and stored, corrupting downstream logic that assumed clean formats.
fix_summary: Added per-field validation in _mergeState(): date must be YYYY-MM-DD and within valid range; time must be HH:MM or H:MM; phone must have 7+ digits (stored as digits only); services must be an array of non-empty strings; lang must be en/vi/es; pendingAction must be booking_offer/modify_booking/null; name must be a non-empty string with at least one letter. Invalid fields are console.warn'd and dropped (not applied) — the prior valid value or null is preserved. Valid fields merge normally.

conversation.content: I want gel manicure tomorrow at 2 PM

Sure! Claude emits bad STATE: [STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":"Gel Manicure","date":"April 15","time":"2 PM","phone":"408","lang":"en-US","pendingAction":"reschedule","existingBookingId":null}] — all invalid fields should be rejected by _mergeState validation.

verify_fix_string: RX-016: field-level validation rejects malformed values
notes: phone field: stored as digits-only string after validation. This ensures consistent Firestore queries (customerPhone='4085551234' not '408-555-1234'). Name validation allows Unicode characters for Vietnamese names. Past-date rejection: dates before today are rejected — if Claude sends a past date, the field stays null and Claude is asked for date again.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies validation strings present in source. Unit-level tests in Group 2 (state-parser.js) mirror the parsing logic but not the validation. Adding validation tests to avail-logic or state-parser would increase confidence. Live API testing required to confirm Claude doesn't emit dates that pass validation but are semantically wrong.
